Hey, this is Dave D. Welcome to the Ultimate No BS Referral Machine where you're going to discover how to generate a constant flow of new business coming from your own customers, clients, or patients. I have with me marketing legend Dan Kennedy. Dan, here we are again. Here we are again. Here we are again with our nice cups. Can you turn that out so the camera can. Okay. Fantastic. So we're going to be talking about referrals for the next four hours. That's all the brain building you're going to see. Yeah, that's exactly it. And how you can generate referrals. I want to start off with a fundamental question right away though is what are the benefits of having referrals other than the obvious one that you know you're bringing in more business. Well, so probably equally obvious. Most people understand that by and large the referred customer client patient is a quote quote better customer client or patient than one that is gotten cold off the street. It's obviously not always true, but if you were going to play the odds, they have more knowns coming in. Therefore, they have less sales resistance. So, you know, depends on your definition of better, but it starts there for people who are not particularly good at selling, who are uncomfortable at selling, who have a staff that's not particularly good at selling. The referred customer client patient is definitely better than one that comes in cold because of the amount of pre-selling that occurred. Let me ask you this really quickly though, because I think there's a misconception that you don't have to sell at all when a referral comes in. Well, you know, a prospect is a prospect. A first time buyer is a first time buyer, yet unconverted to a true customer or client and a customer or client is not yet converted to a champion. It doesn't really matter where they come from. You may be, again, you may need less skill in many situations in order to get the same result. If you're dealing with somebody who came by referral, you may be able to take some shortcuts in your selling process, but it is, to your point, it's very dangerous to make assumptions that you get to have unearned success. The second reason that they're so desirable is because of price and price elasticity. And so the referred customer or client or patient is less sales resistant, therefore they are automatically less price or fee resistant. So you buy yourself a little room to sell at premium prices to present at least premium priced options and have success with them with the referred person versus the person who comes in cold. For a variety of reasons, the trust issue, the fact that they may shop less before they get to you. So the cold prospect, if we generate a cold prospect online, that person probably does not just see us. They look at four or five alternatives of us before they get to us. If you ask me, gee, I got this, whatever problem I got, I got gophers in my backyard. You know, do you know anybody that can make gophers go away? I say, yeah, my garden guy is the greatest garden guy. You may not go look at any other garden guy sites before my guy comes there. And if he doesn't sell you the $15,000 year long no gopher program, immediately you may still not go shop. So it gives us a certain amount of price elasticity. And the reason that's timing critical now is because in many product and service categories, the pool of available customers is actually shrinking. So as are, at least domestically, as our U.S. middle class is shrinking, the percentage of people with discretionary spending for broad categories shrinks as well. So your low half of the middle class has basically gone over a five year period from spending 50% of their income on core necessities. House payment, utility, car, insurance, you know, maybe 10 categories. And having 50% left over to either save, invest, or spend, and most spend. To now spending 70% of their income on core necessities with only 30% left over. So as the pool of available customers and their available spend tightens, it is ever more important to maximize referrals to get the best possible customer you can in the door with the least price resistance. Last, there's sort of a hidden benefit to the referred customer. It means that your existing customer is referring, which makes it all the more difficult for him to leave you. So if I have...
